description:
This image contains two charts. The left chart is a bar chart displaying the CORINE Land Cover (CLC) class distribution in Europe. The Y-axis represents "% of area" from 0.00 to 25.00, and the X-axis represents "CLC class" from 0 to 40. The highest proportion of land cover, approximately 21.5%, falls into CLC class 12. Other notable peaks include CLC class 23 (around 8.5% of area) and CLC class 33 (around 6% of area).

The right chart is a scatter plot showing the density of Persistent Scatterers (PS) per square kilometre for different CLC classes. The Y-axis, labelled "PS/km2", uses a logarithmic scale ranging from 10 to 10000. The X-axis represents "CLC class" from approximately 0 to 45. The data points are coloured black, red, and purple, but no legend for these colours is provided. Five blue ellipses delineate groups of CLC classes with associated land cover descriptions:
1.  CLC classes ~1-6: "Continuous urban fabric" and "Discontinuous urban fabric and other anthropogenic classes". This group shows the highest PS densities, frequently exceeding 1000 PS/km² and reaching up to 10000 PS/km².
2.  CLC classes ~7-18: "Agriculture". This group generally exhibits lower PS densities, mostly between 100 and 1000 PS/km², with some points below 100 PS/km².
3.  CLC classes ~19-27: "Forest and grasslands". PS densities vary widely in this group, primarily between 10 and 1000 PS/km².
4.  CLC classes ~28-36: "Bare soils and bedrock". This group also shows a wide range of PS densities, mostly between 10 and 1000 PS/km².
5.  CLC classes ~37-43: "Wetlands" and "Water bodies". This group generally has the lowest PS densities, predominantly below 100 PS/km².
